supersedes: 2026-07-14 version (values atTop=0.925356, atMidScroll=0.996287, atBottom=1.0) -- rejected: fill was NOT scroll-linked, driven by a 150ms mount-timeout + fixed 1.8s CSS transition. Prior numbers were an artifact of sampling mid-transition against document.body.scrollHeight-relative offsets, never verified against the timeline section's own bounds.
rootCause: app/(public)/process/page.tsx: 'is-filled' boolean flipped once via setTimeout(150), animated by transition:transform 1.8s -- independent of scroll position entirely. QA script also used document.body.scrollHeight-relative scroll targets, not timeline-section-relative.
fixApplied: Implemented true scroll-linked progress: framer-motion useScroll({ target: timelineRef, offset: ['start 0.75','end 0.35'] }) -> useSpring -> motion.i style={{scaleY: fillProgress}}. Marker activation via useMotionValueEvent(scrollYProgress,'change', v => setActiveCount(Math.round(v*steps.length))), each step compares index<activeCount (single top-level hook, no per-step useTransform -- avoids Rules-of-Hooks violation). User explicitly chose this real feature change over a test-only fix.
protocol: confirm timeline bounding rect (#timeline) and viewport height
confirm selected fill selector [data-process-fill] and stage selector [data-process-stage]

sample at: document top (atTop), timeline-section midpoint scrolled to viewport center (atMiddle), timeline-section bottom scrolled near viewport (atEnd) -- all computed from #timeline.getBoundingClientRect(), not document.body.scrollHeight
record window.scrollY, computed transform, and count of [data-process-stage][data-active=true] at each point

passCriteria.startLowOrInitial: 0.1697 -- PASS (clearly not near-complete at document top)

homepagePreview.defectFound: useInView() result 'visible' was computed but never read in JSX; spine fill and step cards rendered fully visible on mount via initial={false} with static animate targets, bypassing scroll-trigger entirely.
homepagePreview.fixApplied: initial/animate props now gated on 'visible' (initial={{scaleY:0}} / animate={{scaleY: visible?1:0}} for spine; initial={{opacity:0,y:24}} / animate={visible?{opacity:1,y:0}:{}} for step cards).
homepagePreview.verifiedVia: next build clean post-edit; included in checkpoint commit c84ff32 per prior session (unmodified this run).

overallResult: PASS -- genuine scroll-linked progression, canonical monotonic increase confirmed with recorded scrollY/bounds/selector provenance
